---
title: "Conservative Estimate of Variance"
description: "Get a conservative upper bound on the error variance for experiment planning."
categories: [Experiments, "Experimental Design", "Inference & Prediction"]
---

::: {.callout-note title="Introduction"}
Use the upper confidence limit for σ² as a conservative variance when planning a
future experiment.
:::

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
pacman::p_load(dplyr, lsmeans)

# load data
trout <- read.table("../../data/trout.txt", header=TRUE)
trout$Sulfa <- as.factor(trout$Sulfa)

model <- lm(Hemo ~ Sulfa, data=trout)                   #fit linear model
lsmeans(model, "Sulfa")                                 #find least squares means
anova(model)                                            #anova model

chi <- qchisq(.95,                                      #confidence level
              36,                                       #degrees of freedom
              lower.tail = FALSE)
sse <- 56.471                                           #SSE
sigma2 <- sse/chi                                       #upper limit for sigma^2
```

The upper confidence limit for a 95% confidence interval estimating $\sigma^2$
would be 2.4269. I would use this variance when conservatively planning an
experiment.


## See also

- [Sample Size](sample-size.qmd)
- [Test Treatment Effect](test-treatment-effect.qmd)

*Data: `trout.txt` — see [sources & provenance](../../data/README.md#experiments).*
